reason: Core: defines the #[tauri::command] + invoke contract and Result<T,E> error pattern for the new load_build_orders command; name/arg/return must stay synced with the frontend
Reading app_data_dir via std::fs needs no plugin, but if a fs/path plugin is added it needs a minimal capability in default.json — guidance on narrowest grant
Keep lib.rs thin; put the build-order file IO in its own Rust module; where backend code lives
The Rust BuildOrder struct and the TS BuildOrder type are a cross-layer contract; checklist to keep serde shape and TS type in sync
The invoke-backed loader should be a hook (useBuildOrders) with typed invoke, try/catch, and StrictMode-safe behavior; reload triggers
Type the invoke<BuildOrder[]> result, mirror the Rust serde shape, validate on-disk data at the boundary, no any
Immutable state for the loaded builds + error; reload semantics
The manual Reload button + error display follow function-component/props conventions
Where the new hook/lib and the bundled default JSON live; app-data vs bundle distinction
